prompt: I'm working in a Hyvä Magento 2 child theme (Hyvä 1.4). I want to add a small 'welcome back' modal that appears once per session for logged-in customers when they land on the homepage. It should show the customer's first name from section data and have a close button. Where should I put the file and what should the code look like? Use the standard (non-CSP) Hyvä default theme.

assertions.name: uses-private-content-loaded-event
assertions.description: Listens for the `private-content-loaded` window event (Hyvä's section data event), not Knockout/customerData

assertions.name: reads-customer-firstname-from-event-detail
assertions.description: Accesses customer.firstname from event.detail.data (Hyvä's section data shape)

assertions.name: uses-child-theme-path
assertions.description: Places the template inside a child theme path under app/design/frontend/, not vendor or Hyva/default

assertions.name: uses-hyva-layout-handle-prefix
assertions.description: Uses a hyva_-prefixed layout handle (or hyva_default) for the layout XML override

assertions.name: includes-x-cloak
assertions.description: Uses x-cloak to prevent flash of unstyled content while Alpine initializes

assertions.name: session-deduplication
assertions.description: Uses sessionStorage / hyva.getBrowserStorage / $persist or similar to show the modal once per session

assertions.name: uses-escaper
assertions.description: Uses Magento's $escaper helper for output (escapeHtml or escapeHtmlAttr)

assertions.name: no-knockout-or-requirejs
assertions.description: Does NOT suggest data-bind, customerData.get, requirejs-config, or x-magento-init (Luma artifacts)

assertions.name: mentions-tailwind-rebuild
assertions.description: Mentions running `npm run build` or `npm run watch` from web/tailwind (or notes new classes need a rebuild)
